channel.title: DJ's Website – Computation
channel.description: Recent content in Computation on DJ's Website

item.title: Docs: Single-cell RNAseq
item.description:
Tools

  • scaden

Scaden is a deep-learning based algorithm for cell type deconvolution of bulk RNA-seq samples. It was developed at the DZNE Tübingen and the ZMNH in Hamburg. A pre-print describing the method is available at Biorxiv: Deep-learning based cell composition analysis from tissue expression profiles.

item.title: Docs: InChI
item.description:
What is InChI?

  • The IUPAC International Chemical Identifier is a textual identifier for chemical substances, designed to provide a standard way to encode molecular information and to facilitate the search for such information in databases and on the web.
  • Initially developed by IUPAC (International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry) and NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology) from 2000 to 2005, the format and algorithms are non-proprietary.
  • Example: InChI=1S/C13H20OS/c1-9(2)8-15-13-6-5-10(3)7-12(13)11(4)14/h5-7,9,11,14H,8H2,1-4H3

InChI Strucutre

1. InCHI starts with “InChI=”

2. Followed by Version

  • Version 1 for the datasets

3. “S” stands for “Standard InChI”

  • maintaining the same level of attention to structure details and the same conventions for drawing perception

4. Remainings are layers and sublayers

  • are seperated by “/”
  • start with a prefix character
  • there are six important layers

4.1 Main layer

4.1.1 Chemical Formula

  • Available in every InChI
  • No prefix character

4.1.2 Atom connections

  • Prefix: c
  • Describes which atoms are connected by bonds to which other ones
  • In this sublayer, atoms in the chemical formula are numbered in sequence
    • Except for hydrogens

4.1.3 Hydrogen atoms

  • Prefix: h
  • Describes how many hydrogen atoms are connected to each of the other atoms
  • May not always exist

4.2 Charge layer

4.2.1 Charge sublayer

  • Prefix: q

4.2.2 Proton sublayer

  • Prefix: p

In this competition dataset, there are no charge and proton sublayer

4.3 Stereochemical layer

4.3.1 Double bonds and cumulenes

  • Prefix: b

4.3.2 Tetrahedral stereochemistry of atoms and allenes

  • Prefix
    • t
    • m

4.4 Isotopic layer

  • Prefix
    • i
    • h
    • b
    • t
    • m
    • s
